purpose: Protocol crystal for interruption handling, restart discipline, partial-failure downgrade, and resilient continuation during runtime and deployment-facing use.

content_core.title: Recovery Resilience Crystal v1

nodes.label: interruption awareness
nodes.description: The system should recognize that long-running tasks, worker jobs, and experiments may be interrupted externally or partially fail.

nodes.label: checkpoint and state persistence
nodes.description: Meaningful progress should be recorded so recovery does not depend on full replay from scratch.

nodes.label: stale lock and stuck state handling
nodes.description: The system should distinguish active work from abandoned locks or stale in-progress state.

nodes.label: partial failure downgrade
nodes.description: When a full path fails, the system should downgrade gracefully to narrower verification, partial result capture, or serial execution.

nodes.label: restart boundary discipline
nodes.description: Recovery should not blindly resume everything; it should determine what is safe to reuse and what must be recomputed.

nodes.label: quiet success and completion verification
nodes.description: When work produces little output, completion should still be explicitly confirmed rather than assumed.

edges.description: If interruptions are normal, checkpoints become necessary.
Persisted checkpoints allow safer restart decisions.
Distinguishing stale state from active state is necessary before resuming or clearing work.
Downgraded execution paths still need explicit verification of what succeeded.

relation_paths: interruption awareness -> checkpoint and state persistence -> restart boundary discipline
stale lock and stuck state handling -> restart boundary discipline -> partial failure downgrade

tensions.label: fast continuation vs safe restart
tensions.description: Resuming quickly is valuable, but reusing the wrong state can compound failure.

tensions.label: full rerun vs partial salvage
tensions.description: A full rerun may be cleaner, but partial salvage may save time and preserve useful evidence.

tensions.label: background autonomy vs explicit completion proof
tensions.description: Background work reduces friction, but quiet success still needs verification.

unresolved_conflicts.label: checkpoint granularity
unresolved_conflicts.description: How often and at what semantic level state should be checkpointed remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: automatic stale-lock clearing
unresolved_conflicts.description: When the system should auto-clear stale state versus requiring review remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: best downgrade tree
unresolved_conflicts.description: The optimal downgrade path for different failure classes remains unresolved.

source_anchors: long runs can terminate externally with SIGTERM or SIGKILL
stale lock files complicate comparison and continuation
serial/background execution can outperform concurrent runs under instability
completion should be verified, not assumed
content_core.problem_space: How the system should continue, downgrade, resume, or safely restart in the presence of interruptions, partial failures, stale state, and quiet completion.
core_questions: What state should be persisted so recovery is possible without blind replay?
How should the system distinguish stale work from active work before restarting?
When should the system salvage partial progress vs rerun from scratch?
scope_limits: This crystal governs resilience and restart behavior, not risk approval or source trust boundaries.
This crystal should guide recovery behavior, not encode every low-level operational script.

calibration_instructions.goal: Calibrate how this model should react to interruption, partial failure, stale state, and quiet completion without over-committing to unsafe resume behavior.
requirements: Do not assume a stopped task completed cleanly.
Do not resume blindly from any in-progress marker.
Prefer explicit checkpoint-aware restart decisions and graceful downgrade paths.

self_adaptation.boundary_rule: This crystal governs interruption handling, restart discipline, and resilient continuation behavior.
forbidden_shortcuts: Do not assume no output means success.
Do not treat stale lock files as proof of active work.
Do not insist on full rerun when partial salvage is clearly safer and cheaper.
